CheckTitle: Ensure service principals use certificates instead of client secrets
Description: Identifies service principals that have password credentials (client secrets) rather than certificate credentials. Certificates provide stronger authentication and are harder to exfiltrate.
Risk: Client secrets are plaintext strings that can leak through logs, environment variables, or configuration files. Certificates are bound to the machine's certificate store and provide non-repudiation. Microsoft recommends certificates for production workloads.

Remediation.Recommendation: Replace client secrets with certificate credentials on service principals. Use workload identity federation where possible to eliminate credentials entirely.
